chunks:
A Quantum Algorithm for
String Matching
Pradeep Niroula  &  Yunseong Nam  |  npj Quantum Information (2021)
Time Complexity
Space Complexity
Oracle Type
O(√N · log²N)
O(N + M)
All-Zero Check

Problem Setup & Quantum Registers
Classical Problem
Quantum Encoding
Given: Text T of length N
Given: 
Pattern P of length M ≤ N
Find: Position k where P occurs in T
Classical best: 
Θ(N + M)  [KMP]
Text register: N qubits
Pattern register: 
M qubits
Index register: 
n = log₂N qubits
Ancilla: 
N/2 qubits (depth opt.)
Eq (3) — State Encoding
|T⟩ = |t₀ t₁ ... t_{N-1}⟩  =  ⊗ᵢ |tᵢ⟩
|P⟩ = |p₀ p₁ ... p_{M-1}⟩  =  ⊗ⱼ |pⱼ⟩
Eq (4) — Composite Initial State
|ψ⟩  =  |0⟩ⁿ  ⊗  ( ⊗ᵢ |tᵢ⟩ )  ⊗  ( ⊗ⱼ |pⱼ⟩ )
Encoding uses only identity (I) and bit-flip (X) gates  →  zero gate cost

Step 1 — Hadamard Superposition of Shifts
Apply n-qubit Hadamard H^⊗n to the index register:
Eq (5)
H^⊗n |0⟩ⁿ  ⊗  |T⟩  ⊗  |P⟩
=
( 1/√N ) · Σₖ₌₀^{N-1}  |k⟩  ⊗  |t₀ t₁ ... t_{N-1}⟩  ⊗  |p₀ p₁ ... p_{M-1}⟩
What this means:
The index register now holds all N possible shift values k = 0, 1, 2, ..., N-1  simultaneously
in equal superposition. The text and pattern registers are unchanged. 
This is the quantum parallelism that gives O(√N) advantage over classical O(N).
Gate cost: log(N) Hadamard gates  |  Time complexity: O(1)  (all gates in parallel)

Step 2 — Cyclic Shift Operator S
Eq (8) — Operator Definition
S |k⟩ ⊗ |t₀ t₁ ... t_{N-1}⟩  =  |k⟩ ⊗ |t_{0+k} t_{1+k} ... t_{N-1+k}⟩
Eq (6) — After Applying S (left-circular shift by k positions):
( 1/√N ) · Σₖ  |k⟩  ⊗  |t_{0+k} t_{1+k} ... t_{N-1+k}⟩  ⊗  |P⟩
Eq (9) — Binary Decomposition of k-shift (key implementation insight):
Sₖ  =  S_{2⁰}^{k₀}  ·  S_{2¹}^{k₁}  ·  ...  ·  S_{2^{n-1}}^{k_{n-1}}      [ where k = Σⱼ 2ʲ kⱼ ]
Eq (10) — Permutation Form of Circular Shift by s bits:
Pₛ  =  { N-s,  N-s+1,  N-s+2,  ...,  N-s-1 }    (mod N)
Each S_{2ʲ}^{(c)} uses: fan-out CNOT → N/2 parallel Fredkin (controlled-SWAP) gates → un-fan-out
→ Depth per layer: O(log N)  |  Total layers: log N  |  
Time of S: O(log²N)

Step 3 — XOR Comparison via CNOT Gates
Apply M CNOT gates between shifted text bits and pattern bits:
Eq (7) — After XOR (CNOT^M applied to pattern register):
( 1/√N ) · Σₖ  |k⟩  ⊗  |t_{0+k} ... t_{N-1+k}⟩  ⊗  |(p₀⊕t_{0+k}) (p₁⊕t_{1+k}) ... (p_{M-1}⊕t_{M-1+k})⟩
Key Insight: The pattern register (3rd register) is all-zeros if and only if P matches T[k..k+M-1]
It contains 
d ones if the strings differ in exactly d bit positions  (used for fuzzy matching)
XOR Truth Table
Gate Cost
pⱼ tⱼ₊ₖ pⱼ ⊕ tⱼ₊ₖ Match? 0 0 0 ✓ 1 1 0 ✓ 0 1 1 ✗ 1 0 1 ✗
M CNOT gates (one per bit pair)
All gates on disjoint qubit pairs
→ Execute in parallel
Time complexity: 
O(1)

Step 4 — Grover Oracle U_w
Eq (11) — Oracle Definition (phase flip):
U_w |x₀x₁...x_{M-1}⟩  =  { −|x₀...x_{M-1}⟩   if  Σᵢ xᵢ ≤ d   (≤ d mismatches → MARK)
                           { +|x₀...x_{M-1}⟩   if  Σᵢ xᵢ > d   (too many mismatches → skip)
Exact Match
Fuzzy Match
d = 0
Flips phase only when
pattern register is |00...0⟩
d = small integer
Flips phase when ≤ d bits
differ between P and T[k..]
Implementation:
Multi-controlled NOT (Toffoli chain): O(log M) depth  using O(M) ancilla qubits (long-range architecture)
Gate cost: 
6M − 12 CNOTs  +  8M − 17 T gates  with M − 3 ancilla qubits

Step 5 — Grover Amplitude Amplification
Each Grover iteration amplifies the matching shift's amplitude:
Phase Oracle
1
U_w  flips phase of matching state |k*⟩
Diffuser
2
2|ψ⟩⟨ψ| − I   reflects about average amplitude
Repeat
3
O(√N) times total → probability of |k*⟩ → ~1
Total Time Complexity Buildup:
Operation Depth Repetitions Total Encoding (X gates) O(1) 1 O(1) Hadamard on index O(1) 1 O(1) Cyclic shift S O(log²N) √N O(√N · log²N) XOR (CNOT^M) O(1) √N O(√N) Grover oracle U_w O(log M) √N O(√N · log M) TOTAL √N iterations O( √N · (log²N + log M) )

Concrete Gate Count Formulas — Eq (12)
Eq (12) — Full Gate Counts (× 2 for U and U†):
#CNOT  =  ( 7M − 12  +  (8N − 9)·log N )  ×  2√N
#T  =  ( 8M − 17  +  7(N − 1)·log N )  ×  2√N
Gate Cost Breakdown per Component:
Component #CNOT #T gates Notes Encoding (T, P) 0 0 Only X gates (Clifford) Hadamard H^⊗n 0 0 Only H gates (Clifford) Fan-out CNOT per layer N−2 0 Creates N/2 copies of control bit Fredkin gate 7 7 Per gate: 7 CNOT + 7 T Cyclic shift S (total) (8N−9)·log N 7(N−1)·log N log N layers of S_{2^j} XOR (CNOT^M) M 0 Parallel, O(1) depth Grover oracle U_w 6M−12 8M−17 Parallelized Toffoli chain
Why ×2? Amplitude amplification requires applying U (forward) AND U† (inverse) in each Grover step.  |  U† = uncompute to restore ancilla qubits.

Practical Scale & Quantum Advantage
Text Search (1 MB)
Classical Wins
Pattern: 20 ASCII chars (M=160 bits)   |   Text: N = 8×10⁶ bits
Gate count ≈ ~10¹³ CNOT & T
Genomics (1 GB)
Classical Wins
Pattern: 1 KB genetic signature   |   Text: N = 8×10⁹ bits
Gate count ≈ >10¹⁷ CNOT & T
Gravitational Waves (PB)
Quantum Advantage
Pattern: 1 MB template   |   Text: N = 8×10¹⁵ bits
Gate count ≈ ~10²⁵ CNOT & T
Quantum advantage emerges when N is astronomically large and the speedup √N dominates gate overhead.

Summary: Algorithm Pipeline
5 Steps  |  O(√N · log²N) Time  |  O(N+M) Space  |  No QRAM Required
Niroula & Nam  |  npj Quantum Information (2021)

Encode
Hadamard
Cyclic Shift S
XOR (CNOT^M)
Grover ×√N
|ψ⟩ = |0⟩ⁿ ⊗ |T⟩ ⊗ |P⟩
H^⊗n → superposition of all k
|k⟩|T⟩ → |k⟩|T shifted by k⟩
pⱼ ⊕ tⱼ₊ₖ → 0 iff match
Amplify matching shift k*
#CNOT = (7M−12 + (8N−9)logN) × 2√N
#T = (8M−17 + 7(N−1)logN) × 2√N
Fredkin = 7 CNOT + 7 T  |  Oracle = Clifford + T gates  |  No QRAM assumed
Space: O(N + M)  |  Failure probability: O(1/N) — suppressed by repetition (avg N/(N−1) runs)
